entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 16
entries.excerpt: In that same year Sarai, seeing her barrenness, said to Abram: “Behold, the Lord hath restrained me from bearing: go in, I pray thee, unto my maid (Gen 16:2); by her perhaps I shall obtain consolation.” And since Sarai was not at rest, although Abram was slow to fulfill her counsel, yet, as it is written, “Abram hearkened to the voice” of his wife (Gen 16:2), and Sarai gave him Hagar the Egyptian, given with the other handmaids by Pharaoh on the day when he took her to wife.…
